flags.note: λόγος — "word" (utterance) vs. "Word" (divine Person in John 1) vs. "Reason/Logic" (Greek philosophical sense). John's prologue deliberately evokes all three.
Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
σάρξ — "flesh" (physical body, neutral) vs. "sinful nature" (Paul's ethical sense). Using one English word across both contexts either over- or under-interprets Paul.
χάρις — "grace" (unmerited favor) vs. "favor" (patron's gift in social context). The patron-client background charges this word with economic and social concreteness.
